summary: Relax AI-coding governance budgets to match agent-operated repository work.
rationale: The previous 15-file PR budget and 600/900-line code limits triggered frequently on legitimate agent-sized changes. The new limits preserve one-layer and red-zone controls while making ordinary agent work less noisy.
line_count_rationale: The base branch governance gate still uses the previous 600/900-line limits; this threshold-change PR updates the existing governance checker and its existing regression test file without reshaping them.
verification: uv run ruff check tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run ty check tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run --group dev python -m pytest tests/test_pr_governance.py -q
uv run --group dev ruff check tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run --group dev ty check tools/check_pr_governance.py tests/test_pr_governance.py
uv run --group dev python tools/check_pr_governance.py --base origin/main
risks: Higher limits may allow larger PRs, so one-layer, red-zone, dependency, config/behavior, and major-refactor manifest checks must remain strict.
The major-refactor soft budget must continue to block very broad changes instead of making file count unlimited.
